title: Marvell Stock at Record Levels With $15 Billion Revenue Target
text: Marvell Technology has set an aggressive fiscal 2028 revenue target of approximately $15 billion, representing significant growth from current levels. This goal follows a pattern of consecutive upward guidance revisions, with management raising its fiscal 2027 forecast to $11 billion, up from $10 billion guided in December and $9.5 billion guided in September. The company's expansion is anchored in its data center segment, which generated over $6 billion in fiscal 2026 revenue with 46% year-over-year growth. Management projects this segment will grow close to 50% year-over-year in fiscal 2028, driven by four simultaneous product ramps: optical interconnects scaling toward 1.6T, custom silicon expected to at least double by fiscal 2028, data center Ethernet switching targeting over $600 million in fiscal 2027, and co-packaged optics technology. The current stock price reflects significant market enthusiasm. Street Mean Target of $128 implies 23% downside, while TIKR Model Target of $487 suggests 190% upside potential. The valuation disconnect stems from analyst targets anchored to older guidance frameworks that management has already surpassed, creating a structural opportunity if the company delivers on its ambitious growth commitments.
